title: Black Coffee on Carpet
timeEstimate: 20–40 minutes; cold flush + vinegar + 3% peroxide on hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet
stainChemistry: Black coffee on carpet is a tannin stain — no milk protein component (that's the coffee-cream version). The tannin grips fast on carpet fiber and gets WORSE under alkaline conditions (baking soda, ammonia, OxiClean alone before peroxide all darken tannin permanently). The recurring traps: (1) hot water (sets the tannin into the fiber), (2) alkaline cleaners as the first step (lock the tannin into a darker brown), (3) saturating the carpet (drives coffee into the pad below where it wicks back as a permanent ring), and (4) chlorine bleach panic moves (strips carpet dye unevenly). Wool carpet narrows the bench sharply — pro from minute one.
whyThisWorks: Cold water blot extracts bulk tannin before it sets. Diluted vinegar's mild acid keeps tannin on the lighter side of its color range and disrupts the fiber grip. 3% peroxide on a damp cloth (hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet) handles residual brown chromophore. Weighted paper towels overnight prevent wick-back from the pad. The honest cap: hot-vacuumed or steam-cleaned coffee on dyed carpet often plateaus with a faint shadow.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): black coffee is melanoidins from Maillard browning + chlorogenic acid + tannin polyphenols. Tannin anchors to carpet fiber via hydrogen bonding. Pro uses cold acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) plus 3% H₂O₂ + acetic acid neutralizing rinse. Sodium hydrosulfite for set chromophore on synthetic carpet.
defaultAssumption: Black coffee spill on living-room or office synthetic (nylon / polyester / olefin) carpet, fresh and not yet hot-extracted. Wool carpet = pro from minute one. Pad bleed-through visible = restoration territory.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Wool carpet = pro from minute one. Synthetic carpet: damp white cloth with COLD water. Press, lift, rotate to clean section, press again. NEVER pour water on carpet — saturation drives coffee into the pad.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 1 tbsp white vinegar + 1 cup cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Vinegar + cool water on damp cloth, press the spot 5–10 min. Damp-cloth rinse with cool water. NEVER mix vinegar with chlorine bleach — toxic chloramine gas.
Hidden carpet area: dab 3% peroxide on white cloth, press 2 min. Color transfers = skip peroxide, vinegar-only finish. Synthetic carpet usually passes; dyed wool carpet doesn't.

spottingProtocol.technique: Bounded oxidation on hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet
spottingProtocol.instruction: Hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet only. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min for residual brown.

spottingProtocol.agent: Weighted Paper Towels Overnight Plus Fan
spottingProtocol.instruction: Stack paper towels on the spot, weight overnight. Fan-dry in the morning. Pulls residual moisture and tannin out of the pile and prevents brown wicking back from the pad.

steps: 1. Wool carpet = pro from minute one.
3. Diluted white vinegar 5–10 min on damp cloth.
4. Hidden-area peroxide test on dyed carpet.
5. 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth for residual brown on hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet.
warnings: Wool carpet = pro from minute one
NEVER hot water — sets tannin into carpet fiber
NEVER alkaline cleaners (baking soda, ammonia, OxiClean alone) — DARKEN tannin permanently
NEVER chlorine bleach — strips carpet dye, doesn't reach pad coffee
NEVER pour water — pad saturation drives coffee into pad
Hidden peroxide test mandatory on dyed carpet
NEVER mix vinegar / peroxide with chlorine — toxic chloramine gas

homeSolutions.agent: Cold and Acid First — No Alkaline Panic Moves
homeSolutions.instruction: Black coffee on carpet is a tannin stain. The recurring traps: hot water (sets the tannin), and alkaline cleaners — baking soda, ammonia, OxiClean alone before peroxide — all DARKEN tannin permanently. Don't reach for those. The path: cold blot → vinegar → 3% peroxide on hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet → weighted towels overnight. Wool carpet = pro from minute one.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Damp-Cloth Blot — Never Pour Water
homeSolutions.instruction: Damp a white cloth with COLD water. Press the spot, lift, rotate to a clean section, press again. NEVER pour water on the carpet — pour-on saturation sends coffee into the pad below where you can't reach it and it wicks back up later as a permanent ring.

Mix 1 tablespoon white vinegar with 1 cup cool water. Apply with a damp cloth, press the spot 5 to 10 minutes. The mild acid keeps tannin on the lighter side and disrupts its grip on the carpet fiber. Damp-cloth rinse with cool water. Never mix vinegar with chlorine bleach — toxic gas.

In a hidden carpet area, dab 3% peroxide on a white cloth, press 2 minutes. Carpet color transfers = skip peroxide. Synthetic carpet usually passes the test; dyed wool carpet doesn't (and wool went to pro at step 1 anyway).
Hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet only: 3% drugstore peroxide on a damp cloth, press 5 to 10 minutes, blot.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners (baking soda, OxiClean) — toxic fumes.

homeSolutions.agent: Weighted Paper Towels Overnight Plus Fan
homeSolutions.instruction: Stack paper towels on the spot, weight with a heavy book, leave overnight. Fan-dry in the morning. This pulls residual moisture and tannin out of the carpet pile and prevents the brown shadow from wicking back up from the pad — the most common cause of 'cleaned' coffee stains reappearing as a faint ring.

steps: 1. Wool carpet = pro from minute one. NO alkaline cleaners. NO hot water.
2. Cold damp-cloth blot — NEVER pour water on carpet.
3. Diluted white vinegar 5–10 min on damp cloth.
4. Hidden-area peroxide test on dyed carpet.
5. 3% peroxide damp cloth for residual brown on hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop on wool carpet (pro). Stop if hot water, hot extractor, or steam was used (often locks tannin). Stop if alkaline cleaner was used as first step (often darkens tannin permanently). Stop if hidden peroxide test fails on dyed carpet. Stop after 2 home cycles plateau.
warnings: Wool carpet = pro from minute one
NEVER alkaline cleaners — DARKEN tannin permanently
NEVER chlorine bleach on carpet — strips dye
Hidden test on dyed carpet before peroxide
NEVER mix vinegar / peroxide with chlorine — toxic chloramine gas

neverDo: Never alkaline cleaners on tannin — darken permanently
Never hot water on coffee + carpet
Never mix vinegar / peroxide with chlorine — toxic chloramine gas
fiberSensitivities: Synthetic (nylon / polyester / olefin) carpet: tolerates vinegar + 3% peroxide on hidden-test-passed
Wool carpet: pro from minute one — peroxide yellows wool, alkaline lifts wool scales
Dyed carpet: hidden peroxide test mandatory
Carpet pad: cannot be cleaned in place — saturation = pad replacement

materialWarnings: Alkaline cleaners DARKEN tannin permanently — don't reach for baking soda / ammonia / OxiClean alone
No hot water — sets tannin into carpet fiber
No saturation — drives coffee into the pad
Wool carpet = pro from minute one

escalation.when: Wool carpet from minute one. Hot water / steam / hot vacuum already used (often locks tannin). Alkaline cleaner already used as first step (darkened tannin). Hidden peroxide test fails on dyed carpet. Pad bleed-through visible (restoration). 2 home cycles plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Black coffee on [synthetic / wool] carpet, age [fresh / hours / days], home-treated with [cold blot + diluted white vinegar + 3% peroxide on hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet + weighted towels overnight]. No hot, no alkaline, no chlorine. [Residual brown / pad bleed-through suspected / hot-extracted suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional carpet cleaner with truck-mount extraction; specialty wool carpet cleaner if wool

contextQuestions: Synthetic (nylon / polyester / olefin) carpet or wool?
Has hot water / steam / hot vacuum been used yet?
Has alkaline cleaner (baking soda, ammonia, OxiClean alone) been used as first step?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Black coffee is the easier coffee variant on carpet — no milk protein component. Coffee-cream-carpet adds the protein step. The most common home failure is reaching for baking soda or OxiClean alone first — alkaline darkens tannin permanently. The second-most-common failure is pour-on saturation that drives coffee into the pad. Wool carpet protocol is pro because peroxide yellows wool and alkaline opens the scales.
